message:
perf: refine atomic orderings, pre-size collections, and eliminate heap allocations

Atomic memory ordering audit across the codebase, replacing overly-strong
SeqCst and AcqRel orderings with the minimum required for correctness:

- Channel sender/receiver ref-counts (mpsc, watch) use Relaxed for
  increments and Release for decrements, matching the Tokio/crossbeam
  convention where the final decrement synchronizes via the mutex it
  takes afterward, not via the counter itself.
- WeakSender::upgrade CAS loop uses Relaxed on both sides since the
  channel data is synchronized by the inner mutex, not the count.
- Bulkhead permit CAS, circuit breaker state CAS, and notify stored
  notification CAS all use Release/Acquire instead of AcqRel/AcqRel.
- Shutdown initiated flag, transport/sink/stream closed flags, NATS
  closed flag, and JetStream acked flag all use Release for stores and
  Acquire for loads (publish-subscribe pattern).
- Test-only counters (fs, lab/injection, virtual_time_wheel) downgraded
  to Relaxed since they only need atomicity, not ordering.
- spawn_blocking fallback thread counter uses Release for CAS/sub.

Collection pre-sizing to eliminate incremental reallocation:

- Vec::with_capacity added to postgres row collection, recovery
  collector/state decoder, gRPC interceptor/metadata, H2 CONTINUATION
  chunks, HPACK header/Huffman decode buffers, epoll reactor token/fd
  maps, connection manager state, supervision plan ops, timer wheel
  waker/expired vectors, and transport aggregator reorder buffers.
- HashMap::with_capacity for epoll reactor state and connection manager.
- ReadyChunks stream reuses its item buffer across poll iterations via
  std::mem::take instead of allocating a new Vec each time.

Heap allocation elimination:

- SymbolCancelToken::to_bytes and CancelMessage::to_bytes return
  fixed-size [u8; N] arrays instead of heap-allocated Vec<u8>.
- SmallVec<[&str; 8]> replaces Vec<&str> for URL path segment splitting
  in web router pattern matching.

Idiomatic Rust refinements:

- CancelBroadcaster uses map_or_else instead of if/else for token lookup.
- RateLimiter uses checked_div + map_or for average wait time calculation
  and caches tokens_per_ms_fixed to avoid recomputing per call.
- Bulkhead::process_queue_inner takes &mut [QueueEntry] instead of
  &mut Vec<QueueEntry> since it never needs to resize.
- gRPC MetadataInterceptor hoists metadata_mut() out of the loop and
  pre-reserves capacity. MetadataPropagator uses explicit loop with
  pre-sized Vec instead of chained iterator collect.
- DNS cache narrows write-lock scope to avoid holding it during stat
  counter updates.
- DeadlineMonitor passes task_type by value (Copy) instead of by ref.
- Transport router dispatch_unicast takes AuthenticatedSymbol by value
  to avoid a redundant clone.
- Metadata derives are adjusted (Default removed where manual new()
  with pre-sized capacity exists); reserve() method added.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -470,7 +470,7 @@ impl CircuitBreaker {
                             .compare_exchange(
                                 current_bits,
                                 new_state.to_bits(),
-                                Ordering::AcqRel,
+                                Ordering::Release,
                                 Ordering::Acquire,
                             )
                             .is_ok()

@@ -83,7 +83,9 @@ impl ConnectionManager {
     #[must_use]
     pub fn new(max_connections: Option<usize>, shutdown_signal: ShutdownSignal) -> Self {
         Self {
-            state: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
+            state: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::with_capacity(
+                max_connections.unwrap_or(64),
+            ))),
             next_id: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(1)),
             max_connections,
             shutdown_signal,

@@ -273,7 +273,7 @@ impl Future for Notified<'_> {
                             .compare_exchange(
                                 stored,
                                 stored - 1,
-                                Ordering::AcqRel,
+                                Ordering::Release,
                                 Ordering::Acquire,
                             )
                             .is_ok()
@@ -309,7 +309,7 @@ impl Future for Notified<'_> {
                     if self
                         .notify
                         .stored_notifications
-                        .compare_exchange(stored, stored - 1, Ordering::AcqRel, Ordering::Acquire)
+                        .compare_exchange(stored, stored - 1, Ordering::Release, Ordering::Acquire)
                         .is_ok()
                     {
                         drop(waiters);
